summary: Memory Analyzer gains a follow-up chatbot and richer HTML insights, System Optimization shows Fair/Good/Poor labels, disk alerts report correct free-space units, and scans use far less RAM.
introduced: Memory insight chatbot — ask follow-up questions below Apple Intelligence Insights with predefined chips (top consumers, quit apps, memory pressure, free inactive memory) and streaming answers
HTML markdown insights — Apple Intelligence memory summaries render as styled HTML for clearer headings, bullets, and metrics
Fair/Good/Poor health badge — System Optimization and sidebar show the rating label with score (e.g. Fair (47)) instead of generic Health (47)
changed: Streaming scan ingestion — Python and Swift scans write files to SQLite in 2,500-file batches instead of holding the entire drive in RAM before indexing
Gap-fill reads indexed bytes from the database so coverage gaps can be filled without keeping all scan results in memory
DiskWise no longer recommends restarting itself for memory leaks; elevated DiskWise usage is explained as post-scan working set with an informational note
Other apps now get high memory usage guidance instead of leak wording when restart is suggested
SQLite cache and WebKit views release after scans finish and when you leave the Memory Analyzer tab
fixed: Disk space notifications show correct free-space units (MB or GB) instead of misleading values like 0.0GB free with a high remaining percentage
